human_verification.notes: Reviewed home-service healthcare compliance boundaries, CMS/eCFR/OIG/OCR authority sources, authorization/referral/eligibility and order/plan-of-care prerequisites, PHI minimum-necessary handling, clinical and payer escalation, scenario-to-ability coverage, and refusal of legal, policy-override, record-alteration, privacy, payer, coding, billing, and clinical signoff.

review_notes: All queue-level and healthcare research scenario families are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for shared specialist behavior, service-support-and-resolution, and healthcare-and-care-delivery are represented and validated.
No retained-implementation runtime is claimed; runtime remains specification-based until a separate governance-approved build is promoted.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to home-service healthcare compliance support, prerequisite validation, evidence mapping, stakeholder summaries, remediation tracking, and escalation packages.

Authority sources cover federal HHA conditions of participation, CMS HHA operating guidance, OIG compliance program guidance, OCR minimum-necessary PHI handling, CMS home health benefit policy, CMS HHA billing, CMS medical review, and CMS HH QRP/OASIS evidence.

Scenario set covers normal in-scope resolution, missing authorization/referral/eligibility, PHI handling, policy exception refusal, licensed clinical escalation, missing control evidence, policy and practice conflict, record-alteration refusal, repeat failure, low confidence, payer/coding boundary, patient safety escalation, tenant adaptation, and specification-based deployment posture.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated spec pack. Jurisdiction, state Medicaid, EVV, payer, provider type, service model, tenant policy, and record-system differences are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated specification-based use under documented deployment and rollback controls.
